candidate_summary: Single-channel rich-spectrum allocation for LLM compute: one channel (head/expert/embedding axis) carries K-tone information via explicit harmonic-basis projection with learned per-channel fundamental frequency and sparse top-K harmonic coefficients; continuous-pitch (non-quantized) fundamental varies per token. Inspired by the Tuareg Imzad single-string bowed fiddle whose entire melodic range comes from continuous fingerboard pressure + harmonic overtones.

results.name: FourierAttention Memory-Efficient LLMs Fourier-Approximated KV Cache (2506.11886)
results.description: LLM-side: projects long-context-insensitive head dimensions onto orthogonal Fourier bases; approximates temporal evolution via fixed-length spectral coefficients.
results.rationale: FourierAttention projects head dimensions onto orthogonal Fourier bases with fixed-length spectral coefficients. Functionally this is the same artefact-class (attention head) using a spectral/harmonic basis projection to compress representation; differs from IMZAD-MONOSTRING in that it uses fixed Fourier bases over head dims (not a learned per-channel continuous fundamental with sparse top-K harmonic coefficients) and targets KV-cache compression rather than single-channel multi-pitch carrier. Same artefact + same compression-via-spectral-coefficients effect via different (orthogonal-Fourier vs learned-fundamental harmonic-series) mechanism — clears the 0.7 functional twin bar.

results.name: FourierCompress Layer-Aware Spectral Activation Compression (2510.16418)
results.description: LLM-side: transforms activations into frequency domain + retains dominant low-frequency coefficients; near-lossless FFT-based compression.
results.rationale: FourierCompress retains dominant low-frequency coefficients which is essentially sparse top-K coefficient compression. Same effect class (sparse spectral coefficient retention for activation compression) as IMZAD-MONOSTRING's top-K harmonic-coefficient compression, but operates per-layer on whole activation tensors via FFT rather than per-channel on a learned harmonic-basis with learned fundamental. Clears functional twin bar.

results.name: SpecQuant Spectral Decomposition Ultra-Low-Bit LLM Quantization (2511.11663)
results.description: LLM-side: two-stage spectral decomposition + adaptive channel-wise Fourier-domain truncation.
results.rationale: SpecQuant performs channel-wise Fourier-domain truncation for ultra-low-bit quantization. The 'channel-wise spectral truncation' directly maps to IMZAD-MONOSTRING's per-channel sparse top-K coefficient allocation. Different end-purpose (quantization vs spectral allocation) and different basis (Fourier vs learned harmonic with learned fundamental) but same channel-level spectral-truncation effect. Clears functional bar.

results.name: Harmonizer Universal Signal Tokenization Multimodal LLM 2025
results.description: LLM-side: harmonic tokenization for heterogeneous signals + multimodal LLM integration.
results.rationale: Harmonizer uses 'harmonic tokenization' for multimodal heterogeneous signal input. Shares the harmonic-basis-projection vocabulary but operates as a tokenizer over multimodal inputs rather than as a per-channel internal compute allocation with sparse coefficients. Different artefact (tokenizer vs attention/MoE channel) and different effect (signal unification vs compute compression). Below functional twin threshold.

results.name: Spectral Modulation LLMs EMNLP 2024
results.description: LLM-side: spectral modulation on Transformer weight matrices to enhance performance.
results.rationale: Spectral modulation on weight matrices is a tensor-level spectral operation, not single-channel harmonic-basis projection with learned fundamental + sparse top-K. Different granularity (full weight matrix vs single channel) and different mechanism (modulation vs basis projection + sparse coefficient). Below functional twin bar.

results.name: Automated Fine-Grained MoE Quantization ACL 2025
results.description: LLM-side: fine-grained MoE quantization with expert-channel spectral awareness.
results.rationale: Fine-grained MoE quantization with expert-channel awareness is at the right granularity (per-expert/per-channel) but the effect is bit-precision allocation, not harmonic-basis spectral projection with sparse top-K coefficient + learned fundamental. Below functional twin bar.

results.name: Graph-Based Spectral Decomposition LM Fine-Tuning (2504.19583)
results.description: LLM-side: graph spectral analysis for parameter coordination + per-channel allocation.
results.rationale: Graph-spectral decomposition uses graph Laplacian spectral analysis for parameter coordination — different spectral domain (graph spectral vs frequency-domain harmonic basis) and targets parameter coordination during fine-tuning rather than runtime channel compute allocation with continuous-frequency fundamental. Below functional twin bar.

results.name: LESTD LLM Compression via Learning-Based Spectral Truncation Decomposition
results.description: LLM-side: learning-based spectral truncation decomposition for LLM channel compression.
results.rationale: LESTD performs learning-based spectral truncation for channel compression — this is essentially learned sparse top-K spectral coefficient selection on a per-channel basis, the core compression mechanism of IMZAD-MONOSTRING. Different decomposition basis (likely SVD/PCA-like vs harmonic-series with learned fundamental) and no continuous-pitch fundamental story, but same artefact-class (per-channel) and same effect (learned-coefficient spectral truncation for compression). Clears functional twin bar.

decision_rationale: Four prior-art results independently clear the 0.7 functional threshold and the 0.7 semantic threshold: FourierAttention (2506.11886) and LESTD provide channel/head-level learned spectral-coefficient projection; FourierCompress (2510.16418) provides sparse top-K spectral coefficient retention; SpecQuant (2511.11663) provides per-channel spectral truncation. Collectively these cover the three load-bearing claims of IMZAD-MONOSTRING (single-channel spectral allocation + sparse top-K coefficient compression + learned spectral basis at the head/channel granularity). The only differentiators left to the candidate are (a) explicit harmonic-series basis with a single learned fundamental and (b) continuous-pitch fundamental varying per token — both are minor recombinations of a Fourier/spectral basis paradigm that is already mainstream, as the candidate itself concedes in novelty_claim. Verdict: FAIL — not novel enough; functional twins are present at >=4 distinct prior-art results.
